nodes.excerpt: full dev environment: WSL2, zsh, miniconda Python 3.13, VS Code, AI toolchain.

nodes.excerpt: TCG collection notes, Shuckle-GX secret rare, Japan trip, wishlist.

Daily log covering caching and database study, including Redis patterns, ACID/MVCC, indexes, query planning, connection pooling, and follow-up system design topics.

nodes.excerpt: Top-level map of Kenny's personal knowledge vault, linking major maps, folders, workflows, and entry points for navigation.

Backlog
nodes.excerpt: full-stack job search platform with live feed, AI match scoring, and Chrome ATS auto-fill extension.

nodes.label: Bobby
nodes.excerpt: personal AI voice assistant (Python, Claude API, ChromaDB, ElevenLabs); currently Phase 11B (vault index).

nodes.label: Bobby Brain Viz
nodes.excerpt: Live 3D vault graph visualizer that turns pushed Obsidian notes into public nodes and links for Kenny's eportfolio while keeping note content private.

nodes.label: Klip
nodes.excerpt: Electron desktop video editor with FFmpeg proxy pipeline and spring-physics timeline.

nodes.label: Onsite
nodes.excerpt: technical interview simulation platform with conversational AI interviewer and debrief (early/active).

nodes.label: Shuckler
nodes.excerpt: Android music app for niche J/K-pop using NewPipe Extractor and gapless ExoPlayer playback.

nodes.label: AI Workflow
nodes.excerpt: multi-model AI workflow: Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, IDE Copilot routing guide.

nodes.label: API Design
nodes.excerpt: REST/RESTful, HTTP semantics, idempotency keys, pagination (cursor vs offset), versioning, auth (JWT/OAuth2), webhooks, REST vs GraphQL/gRPC/WebSockets/SSE/SOAP.

nodes.label: Agent Evaluation
nodes.excerpt: capability benchmarks (SWE-bench, WebArena, GAIA, OSWorld), safety eval (HarmBench, jailbreak taxonomy, dangerous capability evals), deployment monitoring, A/B testing, evaluation flywheel.

nodes.label: Agent Memory
nodes.excerpt: four memory types (working/episodic/semantic/procedural), Generative Agents retrieval formula, MemGPT, multi-agent memory consistency.

nodes.label: Agent Security
nodes.excerpt: security model for tool-using AI agents: prompt injection, capability-based tools, least privilege, sandboxing, memory poisoning, policy engines, audit logs, and Bobby permission modes.

nodes.label: Bobby Commands
nodes.excerpt: cheat sheet of all working Bobby voice commands and known gaps.

nodes.label: Bobby Memory Architecture
nodes.excerpt: ChromaDB + SQLite + Obsidian vault three-layer memory system.

nodes.label: CI CD
nodes.excerpt: CI/CD pipeline stages, GitHub Actions (OIDC, caching, matrix builds), deployment strategies (blue-green, canary, rolling, feature flags), Terraform, ArgoCD/GitOps.

nodes.label: Caching
nodes.excerpt: deep dive: cache layers, read/write strategies, eviction policies, stampede/penetration/avalanche problems, Redis internals.

nodes.label: Claude Code Skills
nodes.excerpt: how Claude Code skills work: markdown files injected into context, gstack architecture (templates + Bun build), gbrain per-project learnings, how to write your own skill.

nodes.label: Cloud Cost Optimization
nodes.excerpt: Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, Spot, right-sizing, Graviton, auto scaling patterns, S3 lifecycle, NAT Gateway mitigation, data transfer costs, tagging, Cost Explorer.

nodes.label: Cloud Networking
nodes.excerpt: VPC, subnets, IGW, NAT Gateway, security groups, VPC peering (non-transitive), Transit Gateway (hub-and-spoke), VPN vs Direct Connect, PrivateLink, data transfer pricing.

nodes.label: Cloud Services
nodes.excerpt: AWS and GCP services: EC2/GCE, Lambda/Cloud Functions, S3/GCS, EBS, RDS/Aurora, DynamoDB/Firestore, ElastiCache, SQS/SNS/Pub-Sub, ECS/EKS/GKE/Cloud Run, CloudFront, ALB/NLB, IAM, CloudWatch.

nodes.label: DSA Practice
nodes.excerpt: DSA patterns by category (arrays, trees, graphs, DP, backtracking, binary search) with spaced repetition notes.

nodes.label: Data Engineering
nodes.excerpt: ELT vs ETL, data lake (Parquet, Iceberg, Medallion architecture), dbt, Airflow/Dagster, Snowflake/BigQuery/Redshift, Spark, Flink, Kinesis, Glue, CDC/Debezium, reverse ETL.

nodes.label: Data Structures and Algorithms Patterns
nodes.excerpt: LeetCode/NeetCode pattern guide covering arrays, hash maps, two pointers, sliding windows, heaps, graphs, union find, backtracking, DP, and tradeoffs.

nodes.label: Database Internals Deep Dive
nodes.excerpt: deeper database internals: pages, WAL, MVCC, vacuum/bloat, indexes, query planner, isolation anomalies, locks, replication, backups, partitioning, distributed SQL.

nodes.label: Databases
nodes.excerpt: deep dive: ACID, MVCC, indexes (B-tree/hash/composite/covering), query planning, N+1, connection pooling, SQL vs all 4 NoSQL types, PostgreSQL specifics.

nodes.label: Distributed Systems
nodes.excerpt: partial failures, CAP/PACELC, consistency models, replication (single-leader/multi-leader/leaderless), Raft consensus, 2PC vs Saga, vector clocks, gossip protocol.

nodes.label: Docker
nodes.excerpt: OverlayFS layer system, Dockerfile best practices (multi-stage builds), networking (bridge/host), volumes, container runtime architecture (dockerd→containerd→runc→kernel).

nodes.label: Dynamic Programming Deep Dive
nodes.excerpt: focused DP guide covering state design, memoization vs tabulation, 1D/2D DP, knapsack, LIS, interval DP, tree DP, bitmask DP, common traps, and practice method.

nodes.label: Economics and Personal Finance
nodes.excerpt: practical economics and personal finance primer covering incentives, opportunity cost, supply/demand, compounding, emergency funds, debt, index funds, retirement accounts, taxes, credit, lifestyle inflation, and career finance.

nodes.label: Engineering Judgment and Tool Tradeoffs
nodes.excerpt: practical guide to choosing tools by fit, failure modes, accepted tradeoffs, monitoring signals, and simpler alternatives.

nodes.label: FlashAttention
nodes.excerpt: GPU memory hierarchy, why standard attention is HBM-bound, tiling algorithm, online softmax, FA-2 and FA-3.

nodes.label: Formal Methods Agent Safety
nodes.excerpt: temporal logic specs, type systems, runtime monitors, NN verification (and its limits), probabilistic verification, neurosymbolic approaches.

nodes.label: Geospatial
nodes.excerpt: geohashing (8-neighbor fix), quadtrees, S2/Hilbert curve, Haversine formula, Yelp-like proximity design, Uber-like live driver matching with Redis GEOSEARCH, H3 hexagons, map tile systems.

nodes.label: IaaS PaaS SaaS
nodes.excerpt: service model spectrum: IaaS (raw VMs), PaaS (managed runtime), SaaS (fully managed app), FaaS (functions), and where the lines blur.

nodes.label: Interview Communication
nodes.excerpt: interview communication guide covering project explanations, STAR stories, coding/system design structure, debugging out loud, clarifying questions, recovery, and resume bullet framing.

nodes.label: Kubernetes
nodes.excerpt: desired state reconciliation, control plane, worker nodes, all core objects (Pod/Deployment/Service/Ingress/StatefulSet/HPA/PV), scheduling, health probes, networking (CNI/kube-proxy/CoreDNS), RBAC.

nodes.label: LLM Capabilities and AI Ethics
nodes.excerpt: opinionated overview of what LLMs can and cannot do, their tradeoffs, whether they replace engineers/humans, and the philosophy, morality, and ethics of AI deployment.

nodes.label: LLM System Design
nodes.excerpt: tokens, context windows, prompt caching (KV cache, TTL, cost math), streaming, tool use/agentic loops, RAG (chunking, hybrid search, reranking), multi-agent orchestration, evals, fine-tuning vs prompting.

nodes.label: MLOps and ML Engineering
nodes.excerpt: ML lifecycle from data and labels through training, experiment tracking, model registry, deployment, monitoring, drift, retraining, and LLMOps for Bobby-like systems.

nodes.label: Mechanistic Interpretability
nodes.excerpt: reverse-engineering transformers: circuit hypothesis, induction heads, IOI circuit, superposition, sparse autoencoders, activation patching.

nodes.label: Message Queues
nodes.excerpt: delivery guarantees (at-most/at-least/exactly-once), Kafka internals (partitions, offsets, consumer groups, zero-copy, EOS), SQS, RabbitMQ, outbox pattern, event sourcing, backpressure.

nodes.label: Multi-Agent RL
nodes.excerpt: MARL: non-stationarity, CTDE, QMIX monotonicity, MAPPO, self-play, league play, emergent communication, credit assignment.

nodes.label: Multi-Agent Systems
nodes.excerpt: multi-agent architectures (orchestrator-worker, blackboard, peer-to-peer), emergent behaviors, failure modes, safety and control.

nodes.label: Networking
nodes.excerpt: OSI model, IP/NAT/CIDR, TCP internals (handshake, sliding window, congestion control, BBR), UDP, DNS resolution, TLS 1.3 (Diffie-Hellman, forward secrecy, mTLS), HTTP/1.1 vs HTTP/2 vs HTTP/3 (QUIC).

nodes.label: Notification System Design
nodes.excerpt: End-to-end notification system design covering aggregation, two-tier pipelines, reliable delivery, the outbox pattern, and a comparison against Meta's production system.

nodes.label: OS Fundamentals
nodes.excerpt: processes (fork/exec/CoW), virtual memory (page tables, MMU, TLB), file descriptors, I/O models (epoll, io_uring), concurrency primitives, CFS scheduling, signals, namespaces, cgroups.

nodes.label: Observability
nodes.excerpt: monitoring vs observability, structured logging, log levels, metrics (RED/USE methods, Prometheus, PromQL), distributed tracing (spans, context propagation, sampling), OpenTelemetry, SLOs/error budgets.

nodes.label: Observability Case Studies
nodes.excerpt: production debugging guide using logs, metrics, traces, SLOs, alerts, postmortems, and case studies for latency, queues, databases, and error spikes.

nodes.label: Process Reward Models
nodes.excerpt: step-level rewards vs outcome rewards: credit assignment, PRM800K, Monte Carlo estimation, test-time compute scaling via MCTS.

nodes.label: Product Engineering
nodes.excerpt: product engineering guide covering problem framing, MVPs, activation, retention, engagement, funnels, user research, prioritization, onboarding, analytics, and project-specific product risks.

nodes.label: Programming Languages
nodes.excerpt: When to use each programming language and why — covering the core mental model (control vs. convenience, performance predictability), plus deep reasoning for every major language from C to COBOL, organized by domain.

nodes.label: RAG and Bobby Vector Search
nodes.excerpt: what RAG is, how Bobby's current index-based retrieval works, and when to upgrade to vector search (~300 notes trigger).

nodes.label: RLHF and Alignment
nodes.excerpt: aligning LLMs: SFT, reward modeling, PPO, DPO (with math), Constitutional AI, steering vectors, reward hacking cases, alignment tax.

nodes.label: Rate Limiting
nodes.excerpt: five algorithms (token bucket, leaky bucket, fixed window, sliding window log, sliding window counter), distributed enforcement with Redis, where to enforce, rate limit headers.

nodes.label: VanossGaming - Dewit
nodes.excerpt: tracks the VanossGaming crew reference where Tyler says \"Dewit\" at 0:53, meaning \"Do it.\".

nodes.id: Resources/References/VanossGaming - Oh I Have a Boner
nodes.label: VanossGaming - Oh I Have a Boner
nodes.excerpt: tracks the VanossGaming crew reference where Nogla slurs \"bow and arrow\" into \"boner\" at 3:13 while Evan trolls him invisibly.

nodes.label: Scaling
nodes.excerpt: vertical vs. horizontal scaling, sharding strategies (range/hash/consistent hashing/directory), cross-shard queries, replication, and tail latency mitigation (hedged requests, LIFO queues, load shedding).

nodes.label: Search Systems
nodes.excerpt: inverted index, text processing pipeline, TF-IDF vs BM25, Elasticsearch internals (shards, segments, near-real-time write path), autocomplete (trie, edge n-grams, fuzzy), vector search (HNSW), hybrid search with RRF.

nodes.label: Security
nodes.excerpt: threat modeling, authn vs authz, session auth, JWT (alg:none attack, refresh rotation), bcrypt/Argon2, OWASP Top 10, XSS/CSRF/SSRF/race conditions, secrets management, security headers.

nodes.label: Security Deep Dive
nodes.excerpt: deeper security guide covering OAuth/OIDC, sessions vs JWTs, passkeys, XSS/CSRF/SSRF, SQL injection, supply chain, container/Kubernetes security, secrets, threat modeling, and incident response.

nodes.label: Software Architecture
nodes.excerpt: architecture guide covering layered, hexagonal, clean architecture, DDD, modular monoliths, microservices, event-driven design, CQRS, dependency inversion, and abstraction tradeoffs.

nodes.label: Software Engineering Tools
nodes.excerpt: full survey of tools modern engineering teams use: design (Figma), IDEs (VS Code, Cursor, Zed), AI assistants (Copilot, Claude Code), CI/CD, observability, databases, and communication tools.

nodes.label: API Design — GraphQL
nodes.excerpt: GraphQL is an API query language and runtime that lets clients request exactly the data shape they need. It is strongest for product surfaces that aggregate many backend entities, but it requires discipline around schema design, authorization, and query cost control.

nodes.label: API Design — REST Principles
nodes.excerpt: REST API design uses HTTP resources, methods, status codes, and representations to expose predictable service interfaces. Good REST design is less about purity and more about stable contracts, clear semantics, and operational usability.

nodes.label: API Design — Server-Sent Events
nodes.excerpt: Server-Sent Events are a simple HTTP-based way for servers to stream updates to clients. SSE is a good fit for one-way live updates where WebSockets would add unnecessary connection and protocol complexity.

nodes.label: API Design — WebSockets
nodes.excerpt: WebSockets provide a persistent bidirectional connection between client and server. They are useful for low-latency interactive features, but they require explicit handling for connection state, scaling, authentication, and backpressure.

nodes.id: Resources/Software Engineering/API Design — gRPC and Protocol Buffers
nodes.label: API Design — gRPC and Protocol Buffers
nodes.excerpt: gRPC is a high-performance RPC framework that uses Protocol Buffers to define typed service contracts. It is commonly used for internal service communication where latency, streaming, and strongly typed generated clients matter.

nodes.label: Anti-Patterns and Code Smells
nodes.excerpt: Anti-patterns and code smells are warning signs that a design may be harder to understand, test, or change than it needs to be. They are not automatic proof of bad code, but they are prompts for closer inspection.

nodes.label: Behavior-Driven Development (BDD)
nodes.excerpt: Behavior-Driven Development extends test-first development by describing system behavior in language shared by engineering, product, and domain stakeholders. It is most useful when examples clarify requirements across roles.

nodes.label: CQRS Pattern
nodes.excerpt: CQRS separates commands that change state from queries that read state. It is useful when write models and read models have different complexity, performance, or scaling needs.

nodes.label: Clean Code Principles
nodes.excerpt: Clean code principles explain how to write software that other engineers can safely read, change, and extend. The practical focus is clarity, intention-revealing structure, and reducing surprise rather than chasing aesthetic purity.

nodes.label: Code Review Best Practices
nodes.excerpt: Code review is a collaborative quality gate for correctness, maintainability, security, and shared understanding. Strong reviews focus on risk and clarity rather than personal preference.

nodes.id: Resources/Software Engineering/Design Patterns — Behavioral (Observer, Strategy, Command, State, Iterator)
nodes.label: Design Patterns — Behavioral (Observer, Strategy, Command, State, Iterator)
nodes.excerpt: Behavioral design patterns organize runtime interaction between objects. They help separate algorithms, events, state transitions, commands, and traversal logic from the objects that use them.

nodes.id: Resources/Software Engineering/Design Patterns — Creational (Factory, Builder, Singleton, Prototype)
nodes.label: Design Patterns — Creational (Factory, Builder, Singleton, Prototype)
nodes.excerpt: Creational design patterns centralize object construction so callers do not need to know every setup detail. They are most valuable when construction has branching logic, required sequencing, or expensive shared resources.

nodes.id: Resources/Software Engineering/Design Patterns — Structural (Adapter, Bridge, Composite, Decorator, Facade)
nodes.label: Design Patterns — Structural (Adapter, Bridge, Composite, Decorator, Facade)
nodes.excerpt: Structural design patterns organize how objects and interfaces fit together. They help engineers adapt incompatible APIs, compose nested structures, add behavior safely, and hide subsystem complexity.

nodes.label: Domain-Driven Design (DDD)
nodes.excerpt: Domain-Driven Design is an approach to modeling software around the language, rules, and boundaries of the business domain. Its most useful ideas are ubiquitous language, bounded contexts, and keeping domain logic explicit.

nodes.label: Event Sourcing
nodes.excerpt: Event sourcing stores every state-changing event as the source of truth instead of only storing the latest state. It enables auditability and temporal reconstruction, but requires careful event schema, replay, and operational discipline.

nodes.label: Event-Driven Architecture
nodes.excerpt: Event-driven architecture coordinates systems through published facts about things that happened. It decouples producers from consumers, but shifts complexity into event design, ordering, idempotency, and observability.

nodes.label: Hexagonal Architecture (Ports and Adapters)
nodes.excerpt: Hexagonal architecture places domain logic at the center and connects external systems through ports and adapters. It helps keep business rules independent from frameworks, databases, and transport details.

nodes.label: Microservices Architecture
nodes.excerpt: Microservices architecture decomposes a system into independently deployable services aligned around business capabilities. It can improve team autonomy and scaling, but it introduces distributed systems complexity that must be justified.

nodes.label: Monolith vs Microservices Tradeoffs
nodes.excerpt: The monolith versus microservices decision is a tradeoff between simplicity and independent service autonomy. The right answer depends on team size, domain boundaries, deployment needs, and operational maturity.

nodes.label: Property-Based Testing
nodes.excerpt: Property-based testing checks general invariants over many generated inputs instead of only testing hand-picked examples. It is strongest for parsers, serializers, state machines, algorithms, and code with clear mathematical or domain properties.

nodes.label: Refactoring Techniques
nodes.excerpt: Refactoring is the disciplined process of improving code structure without changing observable behavior. Good refactoring is incremental, test-backed, and focused on reducing the next change's cost.

nodes.label: SOLID Principles
nodes.excerpt: SOLID is a set of object-oriented design principles for keeping classes and modules focused, replaceable, and easier to change. It is useful as a review lens, but harmful when applied mechanically without regard for simpler designs.

nodes.label: Semantic Versioning
nodes.excerpt: Semantic Versioning is a version numbering convention that communicates compatibility expectations through MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH numbers. It helps package users understand whether an update should be safe, additive, or breaking.

nodes.id: Resources/Software Engineering/Technical Debt — Types and Management
nodes.label: Technical Debt — Types and Management
nodes.excerpt: Technical debt is the future cost created by implementation choices that make later change harder. Managing it means distinguishing deliberate tradeoffs from accidental decay and paying down the debt that blocks important work.

nodes.label: Test-Driven Development (TDD)
nodes.excerpt: Test-Driven Development is a workflow where tests are written before implementation to clarify desired behavior and guide design. Its value is fastest feedback and design pressure, not simply having more tests.

nodes.label: System Design Case Studies
nodes.excerpt: end-to-end design studies for feeds, chat, file sync, collaborative docs, dispatch, payments, video platforms, and job recommendations.

nodes.label: System Design Interview Rubric
nodes.excerpt: 8-phase rubric for FAANG-level system design interviews: requirements, scale estimation, API design, data model, architecture, deep dive, cross-cutting concerns, and evolution.

nodes.label: System Design Notes
nodes.excerpt: scalability, caching, SQL vs NoSQL, queues, load balancing, CAP theorem, rate limiting.

nodes.label: Testing Strategy
nodes.excerpt: testing strategy by layer and risk: unit, integration, E2E, contract, property, load, frontend/backend/database/LLM tests, mocks, flaky tests, and CI.

nodes.label: Transformer Architecture
nodes.excerpt: full transformer internals: tokenization, attention mechanism (Q/K/V), multi-head attention, residual stream, FFN, KV cache, modern improvements (RoPE, GQA, SwiGLU).

nodes.label: Vault Structure
nodes.excerpt: what each folder is for (Projects vs Areas vs Resources vs Maps vs Templates) and the PARA logic behind the structure.

nodes.label: WSL2 Setup
nodes.excerpt: WSL2 gotchas solved: PulseAudio audio fix, Windows host IP, Obsidian portproxy.

nodes.label: Web Animation
nodes.excerpt: CSS transitions/keyframes, GPU-only rule, GSAP, Framer Motion, React Spring, Lottie pipeline, Rive state machines, Three.js/R3F, what makes animations feel good (easing, stagger, spring physics, reduced motion).

nodes.label: What Is Obsidian
nodes.excerpt: what Obsidian is, what it can do, and the right workflow for this vault (discuss with AI first, then write clean notes).
